title: 4. Other Treatment Options in TCM
text:
Herbs
Herbs are very important tool in cosmetics because we can restore the Yin-Yang balance
and internal organ health efﬁciently with them. Various herbs have been used for cosmetic
purposes and Panax Ginseng, Radix Astragali, and Angelica root are commonly prescribed
herbs for aging and cosmetics.
Dry Cupping
Dry Cupping therapy on the shoulder and back is helpful to cosmetic acupuncture. It can
improve  blood  circulation,  relax  tightened  shoulders  and  back  muscles,  and  help  the
gastrointestinal function.
Non-scarring Moxibustion
Moxibustion is recommended for people who have a Yang deﬁciency like a sensitivity to
the  cold,  stagnation,  or  Spleen-qi  deﬁciency.  The  most  commonly  used  moxi  points  in
cosmetic acupuncture are CV-6 and ST-36.
Gua sha
Gua sha is a TCM treatment which scrap the skin with a special device to produce light
petechiae. “Gua” means rubbing or scraping. “Sha” refers to the petechiae: the reddish skin
change after the scraping. The facial Gua Sha broadly is used with cosmetic acupuncture to
help increase circulation, elasticity, and ﬁrmness of the skin.
Acupressure
Acupressure has been gaining popularity as an alternative to acupuncture. We may use it
if the patient feels too much pain on some acupuncture points. Also, it can be suggested for
patients at home after cosmetic acupuncture treatment to pressure some points of face
themselves.
Electro acupuncture
E-stimulation is a good way of giving stimulation on skin and facial muscles. Usage of E-
stimulation on the paralyzed side of face is a very traditional method. In the same way, we
use it together with acupuncture to tighten slackened muscles.

15
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
Meditation
Meditation cannot only calm your mind, but calm your skin too. It helps the patient
reduce stress and get better sleep, which affects the skin condition greatly. We have telomeres
at the end of our chromosomes that get shorter and shorter with aging. Studies have shown
that the increased telomeres activity  and stem cell count in our blood may delay the aging
4
process.  They found that meditation might increase the telomere activity.
5
Therefore, let your patient practice meditation along with cosmetic acupuncture!
Elizabeth A. et al. Loving-Kindness Meditation practice associated with longer telomeres in women. Brain, Behavior, and
4
Immunity, 2013 Aug;32:159-163.
Rao KS, et al. Antiaging Effects of an Intensive Mind and Body Therapeutic Program through Enhancement of
5
Telomerase Activity and Adult Stem Cell Counts. J Stem Cells, 2015;10(2):107-25.

* Fill in the blanks by choosing answers from the box.
1.
Cosmetics in TCM was ﬁrst written in an ancient book,  __________.
2.
If  a  patient  has  sallow  complexion  and  dry  skin,  and  is  troubled  with  bowl
movement, we can expect __________ qi deﬁciency.
3.
Besides cosmetic acupuncture, ________ can reduce stress and may potentially affect
aging process related to telomeres activity.
4.
________  can improve blood circulation, relax tightened shoulders and back muscles,
and help gastrointestinal function.
Answer: 1.(2)   2.(5)   3.(1)   4.(8)
~ !  ~
17
(1)  Meditation                                                 (5)  Spleen qi
(2)  Huangdi Neijing (黃帝內經)                     (6)  Live excess
(3)  Dongui Bogam (東醫寶鑑)                        (7)  Kidney Jing
(4) Electro acupuncture                                  (8)  Dry Cupping
